item.title: CVC’s Maticmind Tries to Calm Bondholders as Founder Probed
item.description: Maticmind SpA said all its published financial information is “complete and correct” as it sought to reassure investors after a fraud probe into the software firm’s founder sent its bonds sliding.

item.title: Crypto Faithful Wins Memecoin Lunch with Trump for $500
item.description: Last year, Morten Christensen spent about $1,200 to attend a dinner with President Donald Trump reserved for the top holders of the president’s memecoin. This year, he’s going to a similar event for just around $500.

item.title: Google Plans to Invest Up to $40 Billion in Anthropic
item.description: Google will invest $10 billion in Anthropic PBC, with another $30 billion potentially to follow, strengthening the relationship between two companies that are at once partners and rivals in the race to build artificial intelligence.

item.title: Euro Zone Price Spike Seen Surging Higher in Second Month of War
item.description: Euro-zone inflation probably jumped in April to the highest in 2 1/2 years as fallout from the Iran war squeezed economies across the currency area.

item.title: Azerbaijan Sold Gold Worth $3 Billion in First Ever Drawdown
item.description: Azerbaijan’s State Oil Fund sold about 22 tons of gold in the first quarter of the year, after a record-breaking rally pushed the sovereign wealth fund’s allocation of the metal to its maximum threshold.

item.title: More Screwworm Cases in Mexico Threaten Cattle Trade With US
item.description: The spread of a deadly cattle parasite is accelerating in Mexico, likely prolonging a halt by the US on live animal shipments and further tightening beef supplies.

item.title: ‘The Jig Is Up’: 70% Crash Marks Quick End to Avis Short Squeeze
item.description: The speculative mania around Avis Budget Group Inc. erupted quickly. It collapsed even faster.

item.title: Pakistan Buys LNG Supply From Spot Market to Ease Energy Crunch
item.description: Pakistan purchased a liquefied natural gas shipment from the expensive spot market for the first time in over two years to ease a shortage caused by the conflict in the Middle East.

item.title: US Government Stake in Intel Has Jumped 300% to $36 Billion
item.description: The value of the US government stake in Intel Corp. has increased fourfold to hit a value of about $36 billion after the chipmaker’s financial outlook showed a resurgence in sales.

item.title: Fuel Shortages Reappear Across Bolivia, Rattling New US Ally
item.description: Bolivia is running out of fuel again, challenging a new government that had vowed to fix the economy after two decades of socialist rule.

item.title: Hut 8 Readies High-Grade Bond Sale For Google-Backed Data Center
item.description: Hut 8 Corp. is considering tapping the investment-grade debt market to fund construction of a data center linked to Alphabet Inc.’s Google, adding to a wave of borrowing driven by the artificial intelligence boom.

item.title: Treasuries Gain as DOJ Drops Fed Probe, Opening Path for Warsh
item.description: Treasuries edged higher after the Justice Department dropped its investigation into the Federal Reserve, potentially opening a path for President Donald Trump’s pick to become Fed chair and push for interest-rate cuts.

item.title: US, EU Reach Critical Minerals Deal to Weaken China’s Grip
item.description: The US and European Union on Friday reached an agreement to coordinate on securing critical minerals and bolstering supply chains, in a move meant to reduce reliance on China for rare earths and permanent magnets.

item.title: DOJ Drops Powell Probe, Smoothing Path for Warsh to Lead Fed
item.description: The Justice Department is ending a controversial investigation into building-renovation cost overruns by the Federal Reserve, potentially clearing a path to confirmation for Kevin Warsh, President Donald Trump’s pick to be the next Fed leader.

item.title: SNB Chief Economist Lenz to Retire, With Brown Set to Take Over
item.description: Swiss National Bank Chief Economist Carlos Lenz is set to retire at the end of the year and will be succeeded by Martin Brown, the current director of the central bank’s academic research institution.

item.title: Standard Chartered Said to Arrange $2.2 Billion Tanzania Rail Loan
item.description: Standard Chartered Plc has arranged a $2.2 billion loan with export credit agencies and development finance institutions for Tanzania’s landmark rail project, according to Turkish contractor Yapi Merkezi Insaat VE Sanayi AS.

item.title: Finma Says Immediate Mythos Access Would Pose Systemic Bank Risk
item.description: Switzerland’s top financial regulator said giving banks quick and easy access to Anthropic PBC’s artificial intelligence tool Mythos would create a severe risk for the country’s financial system.

item.title: DOJ Probe Into Powell and Fed Building Costs Has Been Dropped
item.description: US Attorney General for DC Jeanine Pirro directs her office to close its investigation of Federal Reserve Chair Jerome Powell as the inspector general for the agency has been asked to scrutinize its building construction project costs, according to a post on X.

item.title: French Power Slumps to Lowest Since 2013 as Solar Output Booms
item.description: European power prices plunged to multiyear lows as solar generation surged, flooding the region’s grids.

item.title: US Consumer Sentiment Falls to Record Low on Inflation
item.description: US consumer sentiment fell in April from a month earlier to a record low, reflecting worries around the economic fallout from the Iran war.

item.title: Hedge Fund Launched by Instacart Co-Founder Has AI Call Shots
item.description: Instacart co-founder Apoorva Mehta launched a hedge fund that relies on an army of artificial intelligence agents, one of the few using that technology to essentially replace fundamental portfolio managers.

item.title: Saudi Arabia’s Hormuz Oil Bypass Is Yet to Maintain Full Exports
item.description: Saudi Arabia has achieved a huge boost in crude exports through its Red Sea terminals, but has yet to stabilize flows at its target level for the route.

item.title: Data Center Firm Csquare Files Confidentially for US IPO
item.description: Csquare filed confidentially for a US initial public offering, a signal that more data center companies are lining up to sell shares for the first time over the coming year.

item.title: Texas Ranch Lures Futuristic Startups to Revive US Manufacturing
item.description: The charismatic founders preach a gospel of reviving American manufacturing through frontier grit.

item.title: Intel to Hold Fixed-Income Calls Following Blowout Sales Outlook
item.description: Intel Corp. is set to hold fixed-income investor calls Friday after delivering a sales forecast that shattered Wall Street expectations.

item.title: Nasdaq Futures Jump as Intel’s Blockbuster Sales View Impresses
item.description: Nasdaq 100 Index futures jumped on Friday as a blockbuster sales forecast from Intel Corp. fueled a rally in technology stocks.

item.title: Cava Gets Lone Sell Rating After Fast-Casual Chain’s 123% Rally
item.description: A rally that’s more than doubled shares of Mediterranean fast-casual chain Cava Group Inc. in five months has gone too far, according to its new lone bear on Wall Street.

item.title: Canadian Consumer Spending Rises for Third Straight Month
item.description: Canadian consumer spending rebounded during the first quarter even as US tariffs and geopolitical tensions loomed over the economy.

item.title: India Mulls Options on Iran Port Stake Before Sanctions Kick In
item.description: India is considering various options regarding its stake in the Chabahar port in Iran, including temporarily divesting from it, ahead of the expiry of a US sanctions waiver in coming days, officials familiar with the matter said.

item.title: McKinsey Partner’s Drone Startup Stake Spurs Conflict Concerns
item.description: A top McKinsey & Co. consultant who advises on defense projects around the world has for years owned a personal stake in German military tech startup Helsing.
